key_points: Casas del Rio has chronic maintenance failures and lost mail including SSN cards
AC broken in summer, freezing in winter, cockroaches reported across units
Housing restricts dorm entries to 2 camera points, creating safety blind spots
key_quotes: They always cancelled my work orders. Never got anything repaired all semester.
Don't forget the random cockroaches everywhere
My AC never worked in casas only blew outside temperature air and made a lot of noise. Summer felt like a sauna and winter was straight up freezing.

key_points: Freshmen repeatedly report extreme loneliness and difficulty making friends
Club scene exists but requires active self-searching; not handed to you
Alt, goth, and film communities present especially around arts programs
key_quotes: I haven't met anyone which is so weird because I was popular in high school
Not a friendly college for older transfers and off-campus students.
Literally met and am dating a goth chick here bro bro

key_points: SUB replaced local ABQ restaurant slot with a second Starbucks — students angry
Food theft from communal fridges is a recurring complaint in dorms
Campus bar in SUB had a reported drugging incident
key_quotes: Taking the 'local' spot is insane
I remember when they did the remodel they talked at length about ONLY local restaurants. Then Chick-fil-a, then Subway, etc.
This is the third time someone has stolen my stuff from the communal fridge.

key_points: Students report depression, burnout, and isolation especially in first year
SHAC counseling exists but students unsure how to navigate it under pressure
Campus community is generally supportive peer-to-peer even if admin resources lag
key_quotes: I can feel my depression coming back and I don't like it — I'm already tired, missing class, missing work
go try and talk to shac counseling about your depression before the winter break starts
I feel so alone I feel like all freshman have already made their friends

key_points: New Mexico Opportunity Scholarship offers free tuition for NM residents — real and significant
Apply before December 1 for maximum scholarship access per OneStop staff
Fees can be shockingly high even for minimal enrollment — $763 for one credit reported
key_quotes: If you apply before December 1st and start that next fall, you will have WAY MORE scholarship opportunities
I am literally taking 1 goddamn credit and I have to pay $763.46?
New Mexico has 100% free tuition for all residents at public institutions.

key_points: Individual professors praised highly; quality varies significantly by department
A game design program was abandoned mid-degree leaving students stranded
Graduate academics described as high quality, especially sciences and engineering
key_quotes: Academics are amazing! The educational quality I've gotten here has been much higher than at my previous institution.
the program abruptly ended before I got my degree due to no teachers
Allen Butt's Brain & Behavior course is amazing. Genuinely the best professor I've had.

key_points: GPSA described as rude, slow, and incompetent by multiple grad students
Housing bureaucracy ignores work orders and lost a student's Social Security card
LoboWeb registration system crashes on high-traffic days with no IT support
key_quotes: Every time I call there's a bad attitude. I feel like everyone is so defensive because they know they're doing a bad job
They have lost my mail several times, even losing my social security card and took no accountability for it.
Looks like whole network for loboweb is down, I'm assuming no one from IT systems is in at 7am on Monday

key_points: Albuquerque has highest pedestrian death rate in NM — students nearly hit in crosswalks
Campus itself is considered beautiful with Pueblo Revival architecture and Sandia views
Non-students and homeless regularly present on campus, especially near duck pond at night
key_quotes: NM has the highest per capita of pedestrian deaths in the country. Bernalillo county has around half.
My coworker did get hit crossing university and lomas from T lot. And he had the crosswalk light!
There are quite a few 'non students, non staff' on campus.

key_points: Living in NM limits post-grad opportunity per students — 'you get the short end of the stick'
Strong pre-med pipeline with direct access to UNM Health Sciences and hospital system
Research opportunities exist including paid IRB studies and KUNM radio experience
key_quotes: living in NM, you get the short end of the stick with opportunity anyway… It will always be harder
The medical side's budget dwarfs the rest of the university's.
Every undergrad should hunt for a UNM student job.

key_points: Free tuition for NM residents makes UNM exceptional value for in-state students
Out-of-state students get Amigo and Lue scholarships that meaningfully cut costs
Athletics subsidized $10M/year by state while dorms lack basic maintenance
key_quotes: Didn't you know that UNM would rather spend over $35mil on a new art building and to have state of NM bail the athletics department out 10x over than give their students a liveable environment
I mean the state funds our football program cause it bleeds 10 mil a year.
Sure, its just UNM, its not really selective. But this means a lot to me.

key_points: Politically progressive, diverse campus with strong Hispanic cultural identity
Student community is warm and supportive of each other even when admin fails them
Campus carries a lived-in, scrappy energy — not polished, but authentic
key_quotes: NM is fortunately 'under the radar' when it comes to politics. We are very progressive and diverse.
As an alum, I love this post. Lobos take care of each other!
It's time for me to attend a University and unfortunately this is my only option. Not excited or looking forward to going here but it is what it is.

key_points: **Safety**: campus shooting killed a 14-year-old; campus placed under lockdown
**Housing**: Casas del Rio lost a student's Social Security card with zero accountability
**Drugging**: reported drink spiking incident at on-campus SUB bar
key_quotes: Latest news is one person is dead and one person is injured. Campus is still closed.
I was stalked once and had to call unmpd for an escort and they told me they were too busy.
Definitely go to the university police. They don't serve enough alcohol for anyone to black out so you definitely got drugged.

key_points: KUNM radio station offers hands-on volunteer DJ and engineering experience
Maxwell Museum of Anthropology is free and underutilized by most students
Underground steam tunnels, RPG-style plazas, and a free greenhouse exist on campus
key_quotes: One of the authors of The Expanse graduated from UNM and I found a copy of Leviathan Wakes in Zimmerman.
The Maxwell Museum of Anthropology is free! The UNM Art Museum is too.
The little plaza in Centennial that looks straight from a Boss Arena in an RPG.
